title: sphaeralcea angustifolia subsp. cuspidata
intro: Sphaeralcea angustifolia subsp. cuspidata, also known as the narrow-leaved globemallow or Mexican globe mallow, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Sphaeralcea angustifolia subsp. cuspidata include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with colds, coughs, and sore throats, as well as promoting wound healing and protecting against infections. Sphaeralcea angustifolia subsp. cuspidata can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
